text:
Git branching allows developers to work on different features without interfering with the main codebase. When starting a new task, you create a feature branch, which is a separate copy of the project. This keeps your changes isolated until they are ready.

Once the feature is complete, you can merge it back into the main branch. In team projects, this is often done using a pull request, which lets other developers review the code before it is added. This workflow helps maintain a clean and stable version control history, making it easier to track changes and collaborate effectively.
